topic: Two coworkers discover they went to the same summer camp
raw_output:
[story 1]

Elara bounced in her chair, a bright, almost audible energy radiating off her. The office air suddenly felt charged, like the static before a summer storm. "You won't believe this," she exclaimed, leaning across the cubicle wall, her eyes sparkling with unrestrained excitement. "I just found the old camp brochure. The one from '98?" Liam, across the aisle, looked up, a slow smile spreading across his face. He blinked, a flicker of surprise crossing his features before he managed a genuine grin. "No way. I thought that was lost to the archives." Elara tapped the paper emphatically. "It was 'Camp Whispering Pines.' I remember everything—the mud, the terrible s'mores, the endless creek swims. Are you serious?" Liam pushed his glasses up his nose, a sudden, almost giddy lightness in his step. "Seriously? I was there. I thought I was the only one who remembered that particular patch of woods."

[story 2]

Marcus felt a sudden, almost dizzying lift in his chest as he stared at the faded photograph. It was a blurry shot of a group laughing around a campfire, the flames licking high into the twilight. He felt an immediate urge to move, to stretch, to run. "Look at this," he murmured, his voice unusually high-pitched. He turned to Sarah, who was meticulously organizing files, and her focused expression softened as she took in the image. Sarah’s brow furrowed slightly in concentration, then she let out a soft, surprised exhale. "That... that looks familiar," she said, her tone laced with a quiet recognition. Marcus felt a warmth bloom in his cheeks, a restless energy bubbling up. "It is! Camp Whispering Pines. I was there. I can practically smell the pine needles and the campfire smoke." Sarah dropped her pen, a small, delighted sound escaping her lips. "Me too? I thought I was the only one who remembered the smell of the woodsmoke."

[story 3]

Chloe paced the length of her desk, her movements quick and jerky, her gaze darting around the room as if searching for something invisible. A wide, unrestrained grin stretched across her face, making her eyes crinkle at the corners. "Guys, stop whatever you're doing. I just unearthed something incredible," she announced, her voice ringing with an almost breathless enthusiasm. She held up a crumpled piece of paper, her fingers trembling slightly with anticipation. Ben, who had been quietly reading, lowered his book, his attention instantly captured by her animated display. He leaned forward, his usual calm demeanor momentarily fractured by the sheer force of her feeling. "What is it?" he asked, his voice tinged with curiosity. Chloe threw the paper onto the desk, the sound sharp in the quiet office. "The camp! Camp Whispering Pines! I distinctly remember the whole experience—the way the sun hit the lake, the sheer joy of exploring. I know someone else who was there." Ben’s eyes widened, a slow, delighted recognition dawning on his features. "Wait a minute," he breathed, a genuine, wide smile spreading across his face. "I was there too. I thought I was the only one who shared those memories."
